Title: Comparative analyses of American and Asian lotus genomes reveal insights into petal color, carpel thermogenesis and domestication

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High expression of NnUCP4 is associated with thermogenic tissues and likely contributes to floral heat production.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq time-course; phylogenetic analysis with AtPUMP5; co-expression with AOX during thermogenesis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in carpels and receptacles during thermogenic stages, with expression profiles similar to AOX.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NnUCP4 was one of two highly expressed UCP paralogs in lotus flowers. 2. It clustered with AtPUMP5, a heat-production-related Arabidopsis UCP. 3. Its expression pattern paralleled AOX during thermogenesis, suggesting contribution to heat generation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contribution is inferred from phylogeny and co-expression, not direct functional perturbation.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq BioProject PRJNA748519; published receptacle RNA-seq PRJNA548651

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High expression of NnUCP5 is associated with thermogenic tissues and likely contributes to floral heat production.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq time-course; phylogenetic analysis with AtPUMP5; co-expression with AOX during thermogenesis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in carpels and receptacles during thermogenic stages, with expression profiles similar to AOX.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NnUCP5 was one of two highly expressed UCP paralogs in lotus flowers. 2. It clustered with AtPUMP5, a heat-production-related Arabidopsis UCP. 3. Its expression pattern paralleled AOX during thermogenesis, suggesting contribution to heat generation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contribution is inferred from phylogeny and co-expression, not direct functional perturbation.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq BioProject PRJNA748519; published receptacle RNA-seq PRJNA548651

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: provide respiratory substrates for floral heat production
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High expression of SWEET genes in carpels suggests active long-distance sugar import/export supplying substrates for thermogenesis.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Four SWEET genes were significantly more highly expressed in carpels than in stamens, especially at 22:00 and 2:00 during thermogenesis.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Differentially expressed SWEET genes were upregulated in carpels during thermogenesis. 2. Their timing matched increased heat production. 3. The study proposes that sugars transported from leaves are the main substrates for carpel thermogenesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes transporter family-level evidence rather than one validated single SWEET locus.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: sucrose cleavage to fuel respiration and heat production
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increased expression of most INV genes in carpels indicates enhanced sucrose catabolism supporting thermogenesis.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Five INV genes were identified; most showed higher transcript abundance in carpels than in stamens, particularly during night thermogenic periods.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Multiple INV genes were differentially expressed between carpels and stamens. 2. Most were upregulated in carpels during thermogenesis. 3. Enhanced sucrose cleavage likely contributes to respiratory substrate supply for heat production.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One invertase, Nn5g27613, was an exception and not elevated in carpels.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: sucrose synthase family; SUS domain and glycosyl transferase group 1 domain
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: sucrose metabolism to fuel respiration and heat production
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increased expression of most SUS genes in carpels indicates enhanced sucrose metabolism supporting thermogenesis.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Two SUS genes were identified; except Nn2g14684, SUS expression was higher in carpels, especially during night thermogenic periods.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SUS genes were among the carbohydrate metabolism genes elevated in thermogenic carpels. 2. Their expression correlated with heat-producing stages. 3. The study implicates sucrose metabolism as a major support pathway for thermogenesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Family-level evidence; no direct enzymatic or genetic validation of individual SUS loci.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: vacuolar sugar transport; interpreted as weak contributor to thermogenic sugar supply
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Weak or lower TST expression in carpels suggests vacuolar sugar transport is not the main source of substrates for thermogenesis.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Only Nn6g31664 was significantly higher in stamens than in carpels; overall TST activity in carpels was considered weak.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Two TST genes were identified. 2. TST expression was not elevated in carpels during thermogenesis. 3. This supports the conclusion that sugars for thermogenesis mainly come from leaf-derived transport rather than vacuolar remobilization.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: These were discussed as contrast genes to SWEET-mediated transport.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: condenses two molecules of geranylgeranyl diphosphate to form phytoene, the first committed and rate-limiting step of carotenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: petal pigmentation; contributes to yellow flower color
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher PSY expression is associated with increased carotenoid accumulation and yellow petal color in American lotus relative to Asian lotus.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative metabolite profiling; structural variant analysis; qRT-PCR expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: PSY exhibited higher expression in American lotus petals than in Asian lotus petals during flower bud stages S1 and S2.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as rate-limiting step in carotenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: petal developmental stages S1 and S2
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PSY carried an exon-located structural variant between American and Asian lotus. 2. PSY expression was markedly higher in American lotus petals. 3. Elevated PSY expression was linked to higher carotenoid content and yellow petal coloration.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper discusses PSY as a likely contributor to petal color divergence, but no transgenic manipulation of PSY was performed.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: genome comparison/SV analysis; pigment metabolomics; qRT-PCR; Iso-Seq

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: oxidizes ubiquinol and reduces oxygen to water through the cyanide-resistant alternative respiratory pathway, dissipating energy as heat instead of conserving it in ATP
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: mitochondrial alternative respiratory pathway linked to thermogenesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: drives heat production from respiratory substrates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High AOX expression is associated with thermogenic carpels and likely promotes floral heat production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq time-course; comparative expression in carpels versus stamens; correlation with temperature difference; comparison with published thermogenic receptacle RNA-seq
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Nn1g02044 was consistently more highly expressed in carpels than in stamens, peaking around 22:00 during thermogenesis at 50–100-fold above stamens.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as key control component of thermogenesis, but not formally tested as rate-limiting in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: thermogenic floral stages; day/night cycle; rainy and sunny conditions; low ambient night temperature
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Nn1g02044 was the most abundant AOX transcript and matched previously reported thermogenic AOX1 genes. 2. Its transcript abundance strongly increased in carpels during peak thermogenesis. 3. Expression correlated with floral heat production, supporting AOX-mediated thermogenesis in lotus carpels.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper infers function from expression and prior literature rather than direct gene perturbation in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq BioProject PRJNA748519; published receptacle RNA-seq PRJNA548651

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates/increases transcript abundance of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes including NnANS, NnCHS, NnFLS and NnF3H
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Asian lotus 'China Antique' for gene source; transient assay in white-petaled Asian lotus cultivar
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: candidate positive regulator controlling anthocyanin accumulation and petal color differentiation between Asian and American lotus
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transient overexpression of NnMYB5 induced anthocyanin accumulation in white lotus petals.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: phylogenetic analysis; expression profiling during petal development; transient overexpression; anthocyanin HPLC profiling; qRT-PCR of downstream genes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Among five duplicated MYB genes in the cluster, only NnMYB5 showed high transcript expression during petal development in Asian lotus.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in transient overexpression assay
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: petal development; transient Agrobacterium-mediated overexpression in petals
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NnMYB5 is the only highly expressed MYB among the duplicated chromosome 4 MYB cluster during Asian lotus petal development. 2. Transient 35S-driven overexpression induced anthocyanin accumulation in white petals. 3. NnMYB5 increased expression of several anthocyanin biosynthetic genes, supporting its role as a positive regulator.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The MYB cluster differs between species because of structural variation/repeat dynamics; the gene is proposed to fail to function in American lotus because of absence or inadequate expression.
